Bug fixes for restore drills and flash backups, plus two small UI tweaks.

## Fixed

- **Scheduled restore drills no longer silently fail or read wrong on the dashboard.** (#30, thanks @manilx) Several things were compounding:
  - The scheduled drill shared a per-domain lock with scheduled off-site replication/backup, and grabbed it non-blocking — so when they fired together it silently gave up recording nothing, leaving the dashboard stuck on "never" while a manual run minutes later succeeded. The scheduled drill now waits for the domain to be free (up to a bounded window) and always records a result; a manual drill still returns immediately if the domain is busy.
  - The drill didn't clear a stale restic lock before its check/restore step (the same class of stale-lock bug fixed for tagging in v4.4.1), so a leftover off-site lock could make it fail. It now self-heals a stale lock first.
  - The ransomware scorecard's "restore drill" row read green purely from how recent the last drill was, even if that drill had **failed** — contradicting the red "proven restorable from off-site" pill. The row (and the overall posture chip) now honor the actual pass/fail outcome.
  - Running a drill from Settings now refreshes the dashboard so the result shows without a manual reload.
- **The flash backup no longer includes a `.git` folder.** (#31, thanks @BaukeZwart) If your Unraid flash has a `.git` (from a plugin or a manual git-tracking of `/boot`), BombVault used to back it up while Unraid's own flash backup skips it. It's now excluded from the flash backup, matching Unraid and keeping the backups a little smaller. (A stray `.git` never actually broke the USB creator, but parity is nicer.)

## Changed

- The **ransomware protection card** on the dashboard is now shown only in **Advanced** view.
- The **Advanced** sidebar row is now a checkbox and matches the font of the other sidebar rows.

---
